1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ENGSTROM, MATS ENGSTROM, DAFNE CALIFORNIA ESTATE OSETRA, INC. AS TO DEFENDANT TNC HOLDING KANBAR SPIRITS, INC. RAMIIILAJ DOES 1 TO 10

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS TNC HOLDING, KANBAR SPIRITS, INC. AND RAMIIILAJ'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED MARCH 9, 2012. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077
OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER FILED BY PLAINTIFF ENGSTROM, MATS ENGSTROM, DAFNE CALIFORNIA ESTATE OSETRA, INC.
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-07-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS TNC HOLDINGS, KANBAR SPIRITS, INC. AND RAMIIILAJ'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, SEVENTH, AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTIONS AND SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH, FIFTH, AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE STANDING AND JURISDICTION ARGUMENTS LACK MERIT. THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, SEVENTH, AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTIONS ARE ADEQUATELY PLED, WHILE THE FOURTH, FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE NOT. PLAINTIFFS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF LEAVE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALL OF THE ELEMENTS OF A FRAUD CLAIM INCLUDING JUSTIFIABLE RELIANCE WITH PARTICULARITY, THEIR PERFORMANCE OR EXCUSE FOR NONPERFORMANCE OF THE ALLEGEDLY BREACHED CONTRACT, AND THE RIGHT TO POSSESS PARTICULAR PROPERTY AT THE TIME THAT PROPERTY WAS CONVERTED. WHILE CONSPIRACY IS NOT AN INDEPENDENT CLAIM, IT WILL BE TREATED AS PART OF THE GENERAL CHARGING ALLEGATIONS. THE COURT DECLINES TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE DOCUMENTS SOUGHT TO BE JUDICIALLY NOTICED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LORETTA NAJERA CSR # 5153

APPEARANCES - ATTY. DUNCAN MACDONALD OF ROSSI & MACDONALD LLP, TEL. NO. (510) 522-7910 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. LOUIS J. CISZ, III OF NIXON PEABODY LLP, TEL. NO. 984-8320 MOVING PARTY;
ATTY. KENDRICK D. NGUYEN, TEL. NO. (408) 392-1985 SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED AND WAS CALLED TWICE ON THE RECORD. COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF AS WELL AS MR. NGUYEN WHO IS SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS ARE OBJECTING TO THE WITHDRAWAL AS COUNSEL. ON THE SECOND CALL OF THE MATTER AT 12:15 P.M., IN CAMERA HEARING WAS HELD. Off Calendar May-11-2012 Continued To Jul-25-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jody Weiner, Esq., of Jody Weiner & Associates, Tel. 415.921.2042, appearing for defendants and moving party.
Duncan MacDonald, Esq., of Rossi & MacDonald LLP, Tel. 510.522.7910, appearing for plaintiff and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs TNC HOLDINGS, KANBAR SPIRITS, INC. and RAMIIILAJ's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled as to the first, second, third, seventh, and eighth causes of actions and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action. The standing and jurisdiction arguments lack merit. The first, second, third, seventh, and eighth causes of actions are adequately pled, while the fourth, fifth and sixth causes of action are not. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if leave can do so in good faith, to all of the elements of a fraud claim including justifiable reliance with particularity, their performance or excuse for nonperformance of the allegedly breached contract, and the right to possess particular property at the time that property was converted. While conspiracy is not an independent claim, it will be treated as part of the general charging allegations. The Court declines to take judicial notice of the documents sought to be judicially noticed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: Loretta Najera CSR # 5153
